ks: Building on johnskb: Although it goes against the Heisig **me** thod, this story is a lot easier to remember when you know it's the kanji for "boku." Males usually use either "boku" or "ore" (instead of "watashi") to refer themselves. "Boku" is slightly boyish and soft, "ore" is rough & tough. Mr. T would definitely use "ore" – but when he's one of the husbands hung upside down in a row about to be punished by his wife, he puts on a sweet act and switches to "boku" when he pleads "come on baby, it's **ME**!".

The explanation makes "sense," but is hard to remember because the word " _beginning_ "is too abstract. Back up to the image we used two frames ago - Adam and Eve in their Eden - and try again: The root of all **stubbornness** goes back to the _beginning_ , with two brothers each **stubbornly** defending his own way of life and asking their God to bless it favorably. Abel stuck to agriculture, Cain to animal-raising. Picture these two with their giant, swelled _heads_ , each vying for the favors of heaven, a **stubborn** grimace on their faces. No wonder something unfortunate happened!
ks: A **stubborn** person in the _beginning_ gets something into their _head_ and can't change their mind.

Keep the connotation of the last frame for the word _possess_ , and now expand your image of _shells_ to include the ancient value they had as money (a usage that will come in very helpful later on). hs: The pieces in this character should be easily recognizable: on the left, the element for _part of the body_ , and on the right, the character for _nightbreak_ , which we have just met. What all of this has to do with the **gall bladder** is not immediately clear. But all we need to do is give a slight twist to the traditional biblical advice about not letting the sun set on your anger (which ancient medicine associated with the choler or bile that the **gall bladder** is supposed to filter out), and change it to  "not letting the _night break_ on your anger " (or your **gall** ) - and the work of remembering the kanji is done. And the improvement is not a bad piece of advice in its own right, since anger, like so many other things, can often be calmed by letting the sun set on it and then "sleeping it off."

hc: water; water droplets; water pistol; cliff; ivy; square jewel; soil; dirt; ground
hs: After seeing a constant **horizon** of _water_ , _water_ everywhere for months at sea, could there be anything more delightful to the eyes than to look astern and see the _ivy_ -clad _cliffs_ of land on a new **horizon**? Of course, you'd need the eyes of a stellar telescope to recognize that the vegetation was in fact _ivy_ , but the phrase " _ivy_ -clad _cliffs_ " has such a nice ring to it that we won't worry about such details.
ks: Try to reverse Heisig's story in this Kanji: imagine being trapped in one of those prison towers on an ivy cladcliff like in the movies. From your prison ontop the ivy clad cliff all you can see in the distant **horizon** is nothing but water.

hc: finger; fingers; stone; rock
ks: A huge index _finger_ coming down from the sky is kicking boulders of _rock_ , **clear** ing the land. Maybe it's the _finger_ of God preparing a place for his little people to live.

m: bottle; vial; jar; jug; vat; urn

hc: pack of wild dogs; say; words; keitai; mouth; chihuahua; dog; large; drop
hs: Although we did not make note of it at the time, the kanji for _dog_ is also a low-grade term for a spy. And later (Frame 1517) we will meet another association of criminals with _dogs_. The **prison** here depicts a _pack of wild dogs_ (the long-timers and hardened criminals) into which the poor little c _hihuahua_ (first offender) has been cast. The only thing he has to protect himself against the pack are his shrill and frightened _words_.
ks: The wild dog family visits their cousin in **prison**. They exchange words over the interphone at the visiting booth.

hc: small; little; alien; glass canopy; hood; mouth
hs: Above we see the primitive for _little_ attached to one of those _glass canopies_ you might use to display a family heirloom. The _littleness_ is important, because what is in fact on display is the shrunken, stuffed, and mounted _mouth_ of an **esteemed** ancestor. We may be used to **esteeming** the words our forebears leave behind, but here we also **esteem** the very _mouth_ that spoke them. I leave it to you to imagine a suitable place in your room for displaying such an unusual conversation piece.
ks: An especially **esteem** ed alien has three antennae on his helmet.

v.mn: still more; even more; all the more
besides; furthermore; on top of that

hc: circumference; running track; glass hood; earthenware jar; lidded crock; soil; dirt; ground; mouth; shape
hs: The two primitives here, _circumference_ and _shape_ , belong naturally to the special connotations that differentiate **carving** from _engraving_ (see frame 1639).
ks: Start with a _circumference_ and **carve** away until you get the _shape_ you want.

v.mn: to carve; to engrave; to sculpt; to chisel

hc: mouth; golden calf; horns; heavens

hc: wheat; cereal; hideaway; claw; vulture; broom; heart
ks: This whole kanji is a picture of **calm** ness. The _wheat_ has been harvested and even the _vultures_ sit **calm** ly watching the humans _sweep_ and clean- up after the harvest is over. _Hearts_ are peaceful and **calm** at this time of the year. Even the days are **calm** without storm.

v.mn: disquieting; threatening; turbulent; restless; unsettled; disturbing

hc: craft; snare
ks: **Adroit** : dextrous, deft, skillful. Snare is a little generic so I'll use BEAR TRAP, with huge teeth cartoon style that snares you around the ankle. In the _crafting_ of a _bear trap_ , one must be particularly **adroit**. Otherwise they will become the victim of their down device.

ks: Before the Great Wall of China, there was a dirt **hedge** that spanned across.

The related but less tame key word **seduce** was chosen because it seemed to fit better with the - how shall we put it? - Freudian implications of the kanji. (Observe if you will that it is not sure whether the long slender object is **seducing** the small round one or vice versa.)
ks: A man is easily **seduce** d by a mouth to his "sword.".

ks: All the people that go to the Japanese festivals to catch fish part time are suckers. Me? I'm a pro. I bring a big-ass **golden** **ladle**. I'm a true **ANGLING** professional.

hc: noon; sign of the horse; shovel
hs: Though the meaning has no reference to animals, the parts do: _horse_ with a _mountain_ underneath.
ks: Horses kept in the mountains have a unique savory taste. At least so the text on this **tin can** would have us believe, which contains 100% authentic mountain horse.

hc: rice; shire; cave; soil; dirt; ground
ks: _Condy Rice_ is actually white, but she uses special **cosmetics** made from _dirt_ she found in a _cave_ to appear black - it's better for the political situation.

m: vessels; counter for ships; fish; birds; arrows; one of a pair
hc: turkey; crotch
hs: The key word indicates the Japanese generic term for counting ships. Its elements: _turkey_ . . . _crotch_.
ks: On **Vessels** that travel long distances, sailors often get quite lonely. They keep TURKEYS on the **VESSELS** , but not for use as food. The lonely sailors take turns enjoying the turkey's CROTCH.

hc: flowers; mexican bandit; muchacho; siesta; sun; day; aroma; bound up; spoon; sitting on the ground
hs: **Kudzu**  is a word taken over from Japanese to name the crawling vines that creep up and completely take over trees. Seeing it at its worst in the Georgia countryside makes it easy to understand why it has a reputation as the "plague of the South." From there it should not be hard to arrange the primitives, _flowers_  and _siesta_  into a memorable image.
ks: Flower, siesta: The muchacho was not careful. He passed the warning signs and decided to hold a _siesta_ in the **kudzu** field. The **kudzu** grew so fast, he was completely covered by it in a few hours and not able to escape. Now, the **kudzu** _flowers_ mark his grave.

m: fat; grease; tallow; lard; rosin; gum; tar
hc: moon; month; flesh; part of the body; delicious; spoon; sun; day
hs: This kanji tells us that if you feed the _flesh_ with too many _delicious_ things, it soon picks up a thick layer of **fat**.
ks: Every chef will tell you, **fat** is what make the meat taste delicious.

hc: rake; fenceposts; cabers; rice
hs: _Rake_ . . . _rice_ . . . _fenceposts_. Take special care to draw this character in the same order as the primitive. Note too, that the fourth stroke for _rice_ is already taken care of by the fourth stroke of _rake_.
ks: Even though it's a maddening task, the monks stay **solemn** as they rake _rice_ between the _fenceposts_.

hc: talking cricket; brains; insect; belt; heart
ks: "Don't be **foolish**!" says _Jiminy Cricket_ as he lashes you with his belt. Follow logic, like me, not your _heart_.

m: encounter; meet; party; association; interview; join

ks: Close **encounter** s of the third kind. The space _cadet_ **encounter** s aliens on the _road_.

hs: _Add_ . . . _trees_. Hint: if you ever _played with_ an  " **Erector** Set " or "Tinker Toys" as a child, don't pass up the opportunity to relate it to this kanji's key word and the element for _trees_.
ks: Instant wood. Just add Viagra.

hc: oni; drop; brains; rice field; human legs; elbow
hs: Drop of . . . brains . . . human legs . . . elbow.
ks: A **ghost** , or **oni** , wandering slowly on its _human legs_ , searching for _brains_ to eat. What looks like an _elbow_ here is actually a pictoral representation of the _forked tail_ protruding out of its rear.

hc: caverns; cave; twenty; oven-fire; barbecue
ks: **Commoner** s have to huddle in _caverns around a campfire_ , while higher class people get heating and central air conditioning in a hotel.

hc: moon; month; flesh; part of the body; wind
hs: Ever notice how the **texture** of your face and hands is affected by the _wind_? A day's skiing or sailing makes them rough and dry, and in need of a good soft cream to soothe the burn. So whenever a _part of the body_ gets exposed to the _wind_ , its **texture** is affected. (If it is any help, the Latin word hiding inside **texture** connotes how something is  "to the touch.")
ks: When you expose your _flesh_ to the _wind_ it gets **textured** : goosebumps!

hc: cocoon; clothes hanger; coat hanger

To everybody else it is an _origami_ , but to them it is also a symbol of their **vow** s. – "the _folded words_ are **vow** s". Note : in this story and a few others I use the image of an _origami_ for the primitive " _fold_ ".

ks: The pirate with the _wooden leg_ chased after the _parade float_ and was run over by the next one, leaving **tread** marks all over him and his leg.

hc: mouth; king; jewel; ball
hs: The trick to remembering this character lies in associating the key word with the line from the nursery rhyme about 4 and 20 blackbirds baked in a pie: "Wasn't this a dainty dish to set before the _king_? " If we think of **display** in terms of that famous line, and the _king_ with his head thrown back and his _mouth_ wide open as 4 and 20 blackbirds fly in one after the other, we shall have satisfied both the elements and their position.
ks: The mouth of the king is **display** ing his bling.

m: alienate; rough; neglect; shun; sparse; penetrate
hc: zoo; critters; stop; footprint; bundle; Chaplin; tree; wood; mouth
hs: _Zoo_ . . . _bundle_. Note that the element for _zoo_ is flattened out on the left just as _leg_ (Frame 1372) had been. This is the only time we will meet this form in this book.
ks: [True story] In Osaka the homeless (the **alienate** d) live in large numbers around the city _zoo_. Picture them in winter all _bundled_ up in rags trying to keep warm, **alienate** d from the society, living literally worst than the animals in the _zoo_ who at least are _bundled_ in their natural furs and have pens to live in sheltered from the elements.

hc: sickness; hospital; dart; drop; heavens
hs: Be sure to keep this character distinct from _quick_ (Frame 1799) and _swift_ (Frame 298). Picture a succession of poison _darts_ (the sort that inflict _sickness_ ) flying out **rapid** -fire from a blowgun, so that " **rapid** -fire" can conjure up the proper image.
ks: I picture a sick caveman, and two people **rapidly** blowing darts at him. One is blowing poison darts, the other is blowing antidote darts. Will the sick caveman live or die? Depends on how **rapidly** they fire.

ks: America seeks to _GO_ and _CORRECT_ other nations. But really what they do is **subjugate** them…

hc: stone; rock; ninety; nine; baseball; ten; needle
hs: We begin with the two elements on the right, _baseball_ and _needle_. Since they will be coming together from time to time, let us give the two of them the sense of a _game of cricket_ in which a _needle_ is laid across the wicket. Then imagine using a _rock_ for a ball. A **smash** hit would probably splinter the bat in all directions, and a **smashing** pitch would do the same with the _needle_ wicket.
ks: To **SMASH** a STONE you need to hit it NINE or TEN times.

hc: noh chanting; say; words; keitai; mouth; condor; claw; vulture; king; mountain
ks: **Noh chanting**?!? I meant "NO CHANTING!" Now all you, just shut up! Not another _word_! Don't you know that chanting can provoke attacks from the local _condor_? So, no chanting, and especially no **Noh chanting**.

hc: woman; house; sow; pig
hs: The kanji in this frame demonstrates the traditional Japanese approach to marriage: it is the _woman_ who leaves her family for another _house_ hold, thus **marrying into** a man's family.
ks: Since the Nihonjin man is hardly ever home, the _woman_ feels more like she's married into the _house_ than into a relationship.

v.mn: to marry (of a woman); to become a bride; to marry into (a family)

m: self-effacing; humble oneself; condescend; be modest

ks: A _skeleton_ is shuffling along the shoulder of the road, so you pull up next to him and ask where he is going. He says he's going to donate his **marrow** to the hospital because he got into some financial trouble and lost everything, his **marrow** is the only thing he still _possesses_.

hc: magic wand; augury; cliff; hook; diced; seven; human legs
hs: The kanji in this frame recalls the famous Bengali fable about the group of magicians (the _magic wand_ ) who decided to make a **tiger**. It seems that each of them knew how to make one part of the beast, so they pooled their talents and brought all the pieces ( _diced_ into pieces) together, at which point the fabricated **_tiger_** promptly ate its makers up (the bodiless _human legs_ ). Whatever the parable's significance for modern civilization and its arsenals, it should help with this kanji. Oh yes, we should not forget that cliff-like element. Think of it as an abbreviation of the primitive for _zoo_ (the first and fourth strokes, actually), in order to fit the **tiger** somewhere into the picture. In fact, the abbreviation is perfectly logical, since the bottom elements usurp the room for the rest of the primitive for _zoo_.
ks: Magic wand + first two strokes of pelt ( 皮 ) + diced + human legs. A wizard who turns people he doesn't like into newts with his magic wand goes for variety - he has always wanted a **tiger** pelt - but things go awry when the **tiger** promptly dices and eats him, leaving only a pair of legs. (a fitting end for a wicked wizard).

hc: cliff; grove; tree; wood; sun; day
hs: Look at this character in reverse order, from bottom up. First we see the primitive for _days_ , an appropriate enough way to begin a **calendar**. Next we see a _grove of trees_ growing under a _cliff_. The laws of nature being what they are, the _trees_ would be stunted under such conditions, unless they were strong enough to keep growing upwards until they passed through the layers of rock and soil, right up to the surface. Now imagine that in those little boxes marking off the _days_ on your wall **calendar** , you see that very process taking place step by step: 365 or so time-lapse pictures of that _grove of trees_ each month, from January under the _cliff_ to December on top of the _cliff_. The story is not as complex as it sounds, particularly if you happen to have a **calendar** nearby and can flip through it with this image in mind.
ks: Years-Months-Days: It takes many years for a _cliff_ to form or erode. A _tree_ takes many months to grow. But _days_ pass by regularly - so in this way, we see the movement of time such as with a **CALENDAR**.

Each shelf is dedicated to a certain friend of mine and contains every last one of their presents.

hs: To associate the **peach tree** with the primitive for a _portent_ , recall the famous Japanese legend of Momotarō, the **Peach** Boy. It begins once upon a time with a fisherman and his wife who wanted badly to have a child, but none was born to them. Then one day the old man caught a giant **peach** , out of which jumped a healthy young lad whom they named **Peach** Boy. Though the boy was destined to perform heroic deeds, his birth also _portended_ great misfortune (how else could he become a hero?). Thus the _tree_ that is associated with a _portent_ of coming evil comes to be the **peach tree**.
ks: The pits of a **peach tree** look like _turtles_.

hc: pack of wild dogs; guard; house; glue
hs: One of the worst problems you have to face when you go **hunting** is to _guard_ your take from the _wild dogs_. If you imagine yourself failing at the task, you will probably have a stronger image than if you try to picture yourself succeeding.
ks: Actually when **hunting** it may be a good idea to use a _pack of dogs_ as your personal _guard_.

hs: After the _drop_ and the _glass canopy_ , we come to a combination of three strokes that we met only once before, in the character for _mama_ (frame 105). The pictographic meaning we gave it there has no etymological relationship to this character, but use it if it helps.
ks: I see this kanji as a pictograph of a two story **boat** seen from the front. The _glass canopy_ is the body of the **boat**. The _drop_ on top is the chimney. The horizontal line is the _ceiling/floor_ between the the 1st/2nd floor. Finally, the _two drops_ in the middle are the lights on the decks.

hc: vermillion; drop; not yet; one; tree; wood
hs: That red-orange color we call **vermilion** is found in nature during the fall when the leaves lose their sugar and begin to change color. This kanji depicts the very last leaf on a tree in the fall (the _drop_ hung in the first stroke), the leaf that has _not yet_ fallen as it one day must. Look at its color - vermilion. (Well, not really. The truth is, **vermilion** is made from a mercuric sulfide, but I'm sure you will agree that autumn leaves area lot easier to work with.)
ks: It's autumn: only one **vermilion** leaf on the tree has _not yet_ fallen.

hc: fire; furniture; wooden goods; mouth; tree; wood
hs: **Parch** ment, made from animal skins, was the most common form of writing material used until the beginning of the nineteenth century. When paper took over, a method was devised to make artificial **parch** ment from _wood_ pulp. The _fire_ at the left and in the  "strong" position serves to remind us of the root word, " **parch** ," since nothing dries, puckers, wrinkles, and scorches quite like _fire_. And here is how we put it all together. Take a sheet of paper (a  " _wood-good_ ,"), wet it, and hold it over a _hearth_ in your mind's eye. Now watch as it **parches** the paper, leaving it with a strange and bumpy surface resembling **parch** ment.
ks: Tip: _goods_ \+ _wood/tree_ : This combination of primitives appears four times in Heisig, so it's worthwhile giving it a name. I suggest " _furniture_ " (a common type of _wooden goods_ ). REF: _parch_ (FRAME 215) , [maneuver](../v4/671.html) (#671 操), [winding](../v4/1369.html) (#1369 繰), [seaweed](../v4/2035.html) (#2035 藻). Story: It's been so **parch** ed recently that your _furniture_ suddenly catches _fire_.

hc: cliff; computer
hs: No doubt you will find it in your heart to forgive me for forcing yet another Japanese word on you in this frame. It is not the last time it will happen in this book, but I can assure you they are used only when absolutely necessary. One **_rin_** is equal to about 1/1000 of a yen - or rather was worth that much when it still made economic sense to mint them. While inflation took its toll on this kanji as a monetary unit, it survived with the not at all surprising sense of something  "very, very tiny." The kanji shows a _cliff_ with a _computer_ under it, apparently because it has been pushed over into the abyss by someone fed up with the thing. The total market value of one home _computer_ that has fallen over rock and bramble for several hundred feet: about one **_rin_**!
ks: I threw my **Rin** dows _computer_ off a _cliff_ because it kept crashing. Now, after its last crash, it's worth less than a **rin**.

hc: mountain; Segway; Macbeth; walking legs; bushes
ks: "Walking legs" over "bushes" needs to have a primitive meaning of its own. It appears in several kanji, such as 縫 [sew](../v4/1563.html) (#1563 縫), 逢 [tryst](../v4/2417.html) (#2417 逢), 蜂 [bee](../v4/2687.html) (#2687 蜂), and 鋒 [sword’s point](http://kanji.koohii.com/study/kanji/2768) (#2768 鋒), and it is clumsy to work bushes and walking legs into every story. "Pointy" works fairly well: the walking legs are stuck on top of the pointy part of the bush, and for this character: a **summit** is the _pointy_ end of a _mountain_.

Among its many other meanings are "pure," "tasteful," "quaint," and - get this! - **fish guts**. Since it is a pictograph of a fishhook, it should not be hard to associate it with the key word.

hc: metal; gold; build; brush; stretch
hs: The _golden_ **key** you have been presented by the mayor gives you access to all the _buildings_  in the city.
ks: The _metal_ thing you need in order to enter things you _build_ is a **key**.

m: collar; neck; lapel; one's inner feelings
hc: cloak; prohibition; grove; tree; wood; altar; show; two; small
ks: A **collar** is the part of your _cloak_ that, if tightened too tight, will _prohibit_ breathing.

hc: lightning-bug; schoolhouse; owl; crown; insect
ks: Bugs have to go to school to learn how to become **lightning bugs**. Only the brightest bugs graduate to become **lightning bugs**.

hc: house; head; page; animal legs; eight; sword; dagger
hs: _House_ . . . _head_ . . . _dagger_. Immediately we get another instance of a very odd exception. Notice how the final stroke of the head is lengthened, giving the final two strokes a chance to stretch out and make room for the _dagger_ that fits in beneath.
ks: The new **widow** sits in the _house_ , her _head_ in her lap (the extended stroke), her _dagger_ in her husband.

hc: sickness; hospital; profit; wheat; cereal; sword; sabre; saber
ks: For a country full of pay-per-use public toilets, sudden **diarrhea** is certainly the most _profitable sickness_!

hc: commonplace; cave; rake; screwdriver; utilise; utilize; moon; month; flesh; part of the body; walking stick
ks: To live a **comfortable** life, single men need only a cave for living, a rake for cleaning and a screwdriver for fixing.

hc: soil; dirt; ground; whirlwind; top hat; wind
hs: A _whirlwind_ begins to dig its way into the _soil_ like a drill until it makes a deep **pit**.
ks: A _whirlwind_ digs a hole in the ground throwing out lots of _dirt_ until it ends up with a giant **pit**.

hc: flowers; oversee; retainer; slave; reclining; lying down; one; floor; dish
ks: **Indigo** _flowers_ have to be _overseen_ because they are often picked for their beauty.

hc: shellfish; clam; oyster; eye; animal legs; eight; ten; needle; fiesta
hs: From a **burglar's** point of view, a _fiesta_ is an occasion to takeout the old lock picking _needle_ and break into the unattended safe filled with the family _shells_ (the old form of money, as we saw in frames 84 and 206).
ks: **Burglar** s… those guys!!! **Burglar** s only want money (clams) so that they can get needles (drugs) and party (fiesta)!

hc: finger; fingers; tight; hole; house; human legs; saw
ks: You have trouble squeezing your hand through the hole so you use a saw to enlarge it.

hc: back to back staples
hs: The image here is of _back-to-back staples_. The **mortar** referred to in this key word is a stone or wooden basin used for grinding with a pestle.
ks: I removed all the _back to back staples_ and grinded them in a **mortar** to create metal dust.

hc: paddy-ridge; rice field; brains; half
hs: _Rice field_ . . . _half_. The key word here refers to the **ridges** that rise up between the sections of a rice **paddy**.
ks: A **PADDY-RIDGE** divides the RICE FIELD in HALF.

hc: mouth; shellfish; clam; oyster; eye; animal legs; eight
hs: There is a lot of money to be made of one's **songs** are  " **pop** ular." This is depicted here as a stream of _clams_ spewing out of the _mouth_ of someone performing a **pop song**.
ks: The new generation of **pop songs** have no depth. They just _mouth_ empty lyrics to get _money_. Not like the **pop songs** of my day.

m: cavity; hole; slit; very; great; exceedingly
hc: child; fishhook
hs: Probably the one thing most _children_ fear more than anything else is the dentist's chair. Once a _child_ has seen a dentist holding the x-rays up to the light and heard that ominous word **cavity** , even though it is not likely to know that the word means "hole" until it is much older, it will not be long before those two syllables get associated with the drill and that row of shiny _hooks_ the dentist uses to torture people who are too small to fight back.
ks: The child's arms flailed around at the sight of the hooked tool the dentist was going to use to fill his **cavity**.

hc: mouth; drop
hs: This character is rather like a picture of two of the vertebrae in the **spine** linked by a single stroke.
ks: **Spine** connects head and body.

hc: finger; fingers; consider; old man; slingshot; catapult
ks: In **Torture** 101 class they teach you to Consider the Fingers as the first part to inflict pain to. Then you can move on to other parts (like feet, groin, head, etc. etc. , ) ).

hc: woman; grass skirt; top hat; eight; six; animal legs; celery; scarf
ks: Conversation at a party: A: "Who is that **lass**?" B: "Which one?" A: "The _woman_ wearing the _grass skirt_." B: "Alas, I don't know".

hc: water; water droplets; water pistol; claw; vulture; husband
ks: The Simpsons are on vacation. _Homer_ stops to take a leak in a **mountain stream** but gets snatched by an _eagle_. Hear his scream?

hc: public; eight; elbows; feathers; wings
ks: The **venerable old man** is the _public_ figure head of the Native American Village, answering all questions and solving all problems. The higher in rank you get in this tribe, the more _feathers_ decorate you. Imagine this old man covered in beautiful feathers.

m: bargain; reason; charge; suspicion; point; account; purity; honest; low price; cheap; rested; contented; peaceful
hc: cave; concurrently; animal horns; rake
ks: Stores don't want you to know it, but if you find the secret _cave_ , they run **bargains** there _concurrently_ to their regular prices. (Image: pulling a certain level in a store to find the " **bargain** _cave_ ).

hs: If you play with this primitive's form with pencil and paper, you will see that it begins with a _box_ -like shape, and ends with the final two strokes of the _halberd_ , a convenient combination for the **tusk** protruding from the mouth of an animal.
ks: The kanji looks like an elephant with **tusk** s. the box is the elephant's eye. The final stroke is the **tusk**.

hc: say; words; keitai; mouth; cabbage; flowers; mouth; grow up
ks: Muttering words of gratitude for a wonderful meal, I **discreet** ly slipped my cabbage under the table for the dog.

v.mn: self restraint; moderating one's own behavior; penitence; discipline

hc: eye; juvenile; vase; stand up; computer
hs: Begin with the double meaning of the key word **pupil** : "student" and the "the apple of one's _eye_. " Now all you have to do is dwell on the phrase " _juvenile_ of one's _eye_ " (the meaning here) until it provides you with an image.
ks: In Japanese comics, the eye's of juvenilles all have enormouse **pupil** s.

hc: water; water droplets; water pistol; courageous; courage; chop-seal; hanko; male; rice field; brains; power; muscle; arnold
ks: The _courageous_ man who went into the _water_ to save a drowning victim couldn't be seen, except by the air that **bubbled up**.

v.mn: to well (up); to gush out (of water); to spring out; to surge

hc: hole; house; human legs; sheep; oven-fire; barbecue
ks: Be sure to put a _hole_ in that ceramic _sheep_ before you put it over the _fire_. Otherwise, it'll just explode in the **kiln**. (If you've never done ceramics before, it's important that the object have a hole in it, or this really will happen.).

hc: top hat; protect; person; dumbfounded; mouth; tree; wood; scarf
hs: _Top hat and scarf_ . . . _protect_. Note that the  "tree" in _protect_ becomes a _wooden pole_ here.
ks: _Mr. T_ is **praise** d for his _protecting_ of the trees by being given a _top hat and scarf_ which he wears over his mohawk and chains, respectively.

v.mn: to praise; to commend; to compliment; to speak well of; to speak highly of

hc: whiskey bottle; ghost; oni; drop; brains; rice field; human legs; elbow
ks: The **ugly** _oni_ can't get a date so he drinks _sake_ by himself at the bar.

v.mn: disgraceful behavior; disgraceful behaviour; shameful conduct; shameful sight
ugly; unsightly; hideous; mean; repulsive; disgraceful

hc: thousand; drop; ten; needle
hs: This is the character for the little wooden box that the Japanese use for measuring things, as well as for drinking saké out of. Simply imagine the outside as spiked with a _thousand_ sharp _needles_ , and the quaint little **measuring box** becomes a drinker's nightmare! Be very careful when you write this character not to confuse it with the writing of _thousand_. The reason for the difference gives us a chance to clarify another general principle of writing that supersedes the one we mentioned in frame 4: when a single stroke runs vertically through the middle of a character, it is written last.
ks: It is a thousand or ten? I need my **measuring box**.

hc: bones; one; ceiling; evening; decameron; bound up; sun; day
ks: Think of the early Christian martyrs who suffered **martyrdom** because of their faith. See them held in cells for 10 days (a decameron) before being fed to the lions. They appear before the chanting Roman crowds as _bones_ to be devoured. Think of what was leftover after the lions finished them off….nothing, not even a pile of _bones_ to bury.

kun: わずら.う、わずら.わす、うるさ.がる、うるさ.い
m: anxiety; trouble; worry; pain; ill; annoy; nuisance; irksome
hc: fire; head; page; one; ceiling; drop; shellfish; clam; oyster; eye; animal legs; eight
hs: The existential condition of **anxiety** that arises from the inevitable frustration of our worldly passions is contained in this character. The _head_ is set _afire_ , causing deep torment of spirit (and a whopper of a headache).
ks: An **anxiety** feels like FIRE in the HEAD.

v.mn: worldly desires; evil passions; appetites of the flesh

hc: tree; wood; garter; crown; human legs
hs: Tree . . . crown tied around leg of person.
ks: The guy who was thrown into the river to sink was given one last request. "If I'm gonna be sleeping with the fishes," he said, "can I at least have a **pillow**? I'm real sensitive." The mafia bosses laughed. "Look, my doctor said I can only use _wooden_ **pillow** s, I have a real serious condition. Can I at least die comfortable-like?" The mafia dudes said fine, and let him have his **pillow**. They tossed him the river and drove off…and after a minute, he bobbed to the top, thanks to his floating _wood_ **pillow**.

hc: sign of the hog; acorn; top hat; power; muscle; arnold
ks: _Acorn Power_ is the people's way to **CENSURE** a congressman you don't agree with. Pelt your Senator with acorns today.

kun: お.ちる、くず.す、くず.れる
hc: pinnacle; parthenon; acropolis; possess; by one’s side; moon; month; flesh; part of the body; ground; soil; dirt; ground
ks: Any attempt to _possess_ the _pinnacle_ will **degenerate** into a land (" _ground_ ") war.

hc: well; drop
hs: _Donburi_ is a bowl of rice with eel or meat on it. The character is a pictograph, perhaps designed by someone who felt that the portion of meat he was served with his bowl of rice looked to be no more than a tiny _drop_ in the _well_.
ks: A little one is eating a **domburi** while standing over, looking down into, a WELL. He drops it, and it falls down  & down until it looks like nothing more than a DROP, landing at the bottom of the well!

hc: wheat; cereal; shelf; my bookshelves
ks: The EU and US impose such high **tariff** s on food imports that third world _wheat_ (the most basic of staples) never reaches first world supermarket _shelves_.

hc: tree; wood; float; fiesta
hs: Prior to the use of metal, _trees_ were once cut down and bound together for use as **scaffolding** material. In the case of this kanji, what is being constructed is not a skyscraper but a simple _float_.
ks: Japanese festival _floats_ are often so tall they need _wood_ **scaffolds** to be completed. (<http://www.japan-guide.com/e/e5909.html>).

When sitting through laser eye surgery, the surgeon _stretches_ open your _eye_ with a tight-fitting _tool_. The thought of a laser cutting through the lens of your eye within moments, and possibly blinding you in the case of a mishap, makes for a very **disquieting** _state of mind_.

hc: woman; zoo; critters; moon; month; flesh; part of the body
hs: What makes a man a **bridegroom** is obviously a _woman_ and her dowry, here presented as a small _zoo_ (animals were often used for this purpose in earlier societies) and a _month_ away from it all (the  "honey _moon_ ").
ks: The virgin **bridegroom** tries to understand the new, mysterious _part of_ his _woman's body_ \- but with little success. It's a _zoo_ down there! (primitives placement: the part of the body is 'down there' - the woman is 'left' unsatisfied).

m: pining; yearn for; love dearly; adore
hc: graveyard; flowers; sun; day; large; St. Bernard; valentine
hs: _Graveyard_ . . . _valentine_. Note carefully the stroke order of the _valentine_ primitive.
ks: **Pining** , yearn for… etc. Presumably for lost love. An easy one here, someone standing in the _graveyard_ , placing an old _valentine_ card amonst all the existing tributes.

v.mn: to yearn for; to long for; to pine for; to miss; to love dearly; to adore

hc: eye; cross-eyed; net; ability; elbow; moon; month; flesh; part of the body; spoon; sitting on the ground
ks: Hey, screw you! Don't you _look_ down on me! I have the _ability_ to **quit** anytime I want!

m: rectify; straighten; correct; reform; cure; control; pretend; falsify
hc: dart; drop; heavens; angel; heavens; mouth; hood; mouth
ks: _Angels_ have now been given a powerful tool to **rectify** human behavior instantly, without delay - they have been allowed to drop _darts_ on those who misbehave… spare the _dart_ and spoil the man…

v.mn: correction (of a fault, defect, flaw, etc.); remedy; rectification; redress; ref

m: so-and-so; one; a certain; that person
hc: sweet; wicker basket; picnic basket; tree; wood
hs: The key word here refers to the adjective for an unspecified person or thing. Its elements: _wicker basket_ . . . _tree_.
ks: I had the same idea as fuabrisu. A generic person is described here as having body sort of like a tree trunk and head sort of like a wicker basket.

hc: pent in; person
ks: A person _pent in_ is obviously **captured**. Imagine a man put inside a wooden box, his evil capturers waiting for ransom money.

v.mn: to be caught; to be captured; to be taken prisoner; to be arrested; to be appreh

hc: insect; craft
ks: Little is it known that a **rainbow** is actually created by _insects_ of different colours flying in formation, a _craft_ they have perfected to create the **rainbow** s we see today.

m: ooze; flow; soak in; penetrate; secrete
hc: water; water droplets; water pistol; invariably; heart; stick; drop; fishhook
ks: Anything full of _water_ will _invariably_ begin to **ooze**.

kun: ようや.く、やや、ようよ.う、すす.む
hc: water; water droplets; water pistol; executioner; chop off; car; axe
ks: I imagine firemen working **steadily** to rescue a man from a crashed _car_. First, they spray it with _water_ , then, they use an _axe_ to break through the window.

kun: かしわ、すす.める、そな.える
hc: moon; month; flesh; part of the body; virtuous; sheep; horns; mouth
ks: The _virtuous_ vegan will not touch this **dining tray** because it has had a piece of _meat_ on it.

hc: insect; sentence; sentence; plaid; kilt; Billy Connolly
ks: The **mosquito** (an _insect_ ) knows only one _sentence_ : Bzzzzz.

hc: cliff; fingerprint
ks: Seeing some finger prints at the edge of a cliff, I wondered who that **unlucky** person was.

hc: flowers; water; water droplets; water pistol; furniture; goods; mouth; tree; wood
ks: Let _wood_ en _goods_ = _furniture_. Then: Walking along the sea shore you see some washed-up _furniture_ with _water_ (waves) splashing against it. From a distance it looks like _flowers_ are growing out the top, but as you get closer you see it is in fact **seaweed**.

hc: woman; antique; vase; stand up; hood; old; tombstone; gravestone; church; ten; needle; mouth
hs: The phrase **legitimate wife** would have no meaning if there were not such things as  "illegitimate wives," taken because one's legal _woman_ has turned into an _antique_. The very offense of the idea should help you remember the kanji.
ks: Legitmate wifes tend to be women who are antiques in contrast to the young bit of illigitmate fluff on the side.

kun: まじな.う、のろ.い、まじな.い、のろ.う
hc: mouth; elder brother; teenager; mouth; human legs
hs: For some reason, the inventor of this kanji associated a **curse** with the _mouth_ of an _older brother_. I leave it to you to decide if he is on the giving or receiving end of the sorcery.
ks: Caution: this means a spell, not to swear. I put a **curse** on my _elder brother_ so that he can never open his _mouth_.

v.mn: to curse; to put a curse on; to detest intensely

hc: executioner; car; axe
ks: I wanted a convertible so I **chopped off** the top of my _car_ with an _axe_.

v.mn: to kill (a human) using a blade (sword, machete, knife, etc.); to slice (off); t

kun: うら.む、うらみ、うら.めしい
hc: mailbox; evening; fingerprint; heart
ks: I couldn't drop my **grudge** against him. So I put a severed pig's _heart_ in his _mailbox_. He got the message.

hc: kebab; in; stick; mouth
hs: This pictograph of two pieces of meat on a skewer, a **shish kebab** , will help us in the next frame.
ks: This is a pictograph if ever I saw one. Two chunks of meat on a skewer.

hc: menacing; mouth; incandescent; red
ks: To **upbraid** someone, you criticize them in a menacing way. In other words, you jaw at them with your MOUTH until both sides of their face turn RED (hence the double-red).

hs: Maybe this helps you see how the Japanese have no trouble keeping **convex** distinct from _concave_. Note the odd feeling of the third stroke. If it doesn't feel all that strange now, by the time you are done with this book, it will. There are very few times you will have to write it.
ks: When I get _vex_ ed, I give people the middle finger.

kun: は.れる、は.れ、は.らす、はれもの
hc: tumour; moon; month; flesh; part of the body; heavy; thousand; ri; computer
ks: A **tumor** is a useless _body part_ that is nothing but _heavy_.

v.mn: to swell (from inflammation); to become swollen

hc: sound; vase; stand up; sun; day; employee; mouth; shellfish; clam; oyster; eye; animal legs; eight
hs: Poetry restricted to verses that **rhyme** often finds it has to abandon clarity of thought in order to make the **rhyme** of the words work. In this kanji's picture, one becomes a kind of  " _sound-employee_. "
ks: What is a **RHYME** but some SOUND that EMPLOYEES say to get them through work.

v.mn: reverberation; swelling (of a hymn); trailing note

hc: rain; weather; inter; Inter Milan; tree; wood; eye
hs: Think of **frost** as a cooperative venture, an _inter_ -action of the malevolent forces of _weather_ that sit around a conference table and finally decide to allow a very light amount of moisture to fall just before a short and sudden freeze.
ks: **FROST** is kind of an _inter_ - _weather_ , phenomenon, something between rain, snow and freezing temp’s.

hc: eat; food; umbrella; good; halo; drop; silver; puzzle; horns; two hands
hs: _Mochi_ is the glutinous rice the Japanese pound into cakes. Its primitives are: _food_ . . . _puzzle_.
ks: Call your friend and he answers the phone "Mochi, **mochi**!" whatever he is _eating_ sure is making it hard to understand. I will _puzzle_ over the content of our conversation for some time to come.

hc: stone; rock; resemblance; spark; candle; small; little; moon; month; flesh; part of the body
hs: The word **nitrate** should immediately suggest a beaker of **nitric** acid, which, as every high-school chemistry student knows, can eat its way through some pretty tough substances. Here we imagine pouring it over a _rock_ and watching the _sparks_ fly as it bores a hole through the rock.
ks: That stone has sparks coming out of it! Someone must have poured **nitrate** acid on it.

hc: bamboo; someone; puppet; sooty; old man; sun; day
ks: I've always wanted to use _bamboo_ **chopsticks** to eat sushi off a naked woman. Unfortunately, there is no woman in my life, so this _love doll_ will have to do.

kun: いまし.める、みことのり
hc: bundle; Chaplin; tree; wood; mouth; power; muscle; arnold
hs: In order to keep this character distinct from that for an _imperial edict_ (Frame 366), we must draw again on a pun. Think of the **order** here as a mail **order** or an **order** of pizza phoned in by the Emperor for delivery to the **imperial** palace. Then it will not be hard to put together _bundle_ and _muscle_ to form a story about an **imperial order**.
ks: This is an **imperial order** for pizza. To deliver the huge bundle of pizzas to the emperor, the pizza boy needs huge muscles.

hc: tree; wood; bureaucrat; house; maestro without baton
ks: An excellent use of _wood_ is to make **coffin** s for _bureaucrat_ s.

hc: confucian; person; demand; rain; weather; comb
ks: No more **Confucian** jibba-jabba! I _demand_ you tell me why it's _raining_ _combs_!".

hc: king; jewel; ball; sentence; plaid; kilt; Billy Connolly
ks: Two kings, kilt: After an hour in the onsen, the two scottish _kings_ , both heavily **speckled** like the scottish are wont to, decide to get out. However, in the mean time one of their _kilts_ was stolen and only one was left.

hs: Of the many and widely different meanings of this kanji, we select the verbal meaning of **clasp** , which fits in well with its primitive elements: _tenacious_ . . . _hand_.
ks: _Tenaciously_ **clasping** something with your hand.

hc: autumn; wheat; cereal; fire; heart
ks: In AUTUMN, the farmer's HEART is in **DISTRESS** as to if he has enough crops. Even better, the farmer will experience great **DISTRESS** in his HEART if his WHEAT catches FIRE!

hc: tree; wood; rice; woman
ks: The young taskmaster (see [number](../v4/929.html) (#929 数)) uses a _tree_ as a **watchtower** to get a better view of his _rice_ -carrying _women_. "I can seeeee you! One! Two! Three!".

hc: flag; stick; eye
hs: The _flag_ here has an extra vertical stroke in it. Think of it as an **eyebrow** pencil stuck in the _eye_.
ks: The **eyebrow** s hover over the _eyes_ like a _divided flag_.

hc: fuel; flowers; new; red pepper; stand up; vase; tree; wood; axe
ks: If your **firewood** is too _new_ and still has _flowers_ on it, it's unsuitable for burning. You need drier wood.

v.mn: piece(s) of firewood (esp. chopped or split from logs)

hc: soil; dirt; ground; turkey
ks: After our pre-Thanksgiving massacre, we **piled high** all the _turkey_ corpses atop the _soil_ of the farmyard.

hc: cloak; mexican bandit; muchacho; siesta; sun; day; aroma; bound up; spoon; sitting on the ground
hs: The color of the serape or _cloak_ of our muchacho on _siesta_ is a dull **brown** , the color this kanji indicates.
ks: Why is my package late? Imagine a UPS driver dressed in **BROWN** _garments_ taking a _siesta_. What can **BROWN** do for you?

v.mn: dark brown; colour of tanned skin

kun: たまわ.る、たま.う、たも.う
hc: shellfish; clam; oyster; eye; animal legs; eight; easy; sun; day; knot; piglet’s tail
ks: A **grant** is _easy money_.

m: darning; repair; mend; trim; tidy up; adjust
hc: thread; spiderman; virtuous; sheep; horns; mouth
ks: After a day being _virtuouos_ and keeping the city safe, _Spiderman_ 's outfit is in need of some repair. Luckily, as he can shoot out threads **darning** is very easy for him to do.

v.mn: to mend; to patch up; to repair; to fix; to darn

hc: tree; wood; whole; umbrella; king; jewel; ball
hs: Here we think of **plug** in the sense of a cork or stopper used to seal the mouth of a bottle, water faucet, or something with liquid running out of it. Forgetting the abstract picture of the former frame, let us work with all the primitive units: _tree_ . . . _umbrella_ . . . _ball_. Imagine a _tree_ with a faucet in the side out of which tennis _balls_ are flowing, bouncing all over the ground by the hundreds. You fight your way up to it and shove your giant beach _umbrella_ into the _tree_ to **plug** it up.
ks: The fat _king_ under his _umbrella_ offers useless advice to the workers. "You'll need the _whole_ _tree_ to **plug** that gap". Imagine a grossly fat, incompetent king under a parasol giving annoying advice to sweating workers who know what they're doing. (I always have trouble bringing up the "whole" primitive. This story reminds me how to build that part. ).

hc: flowers; heart
ks: Giving a girl _flowers_ is like lighting the **wick** to her _heart_.

v.mn: wick; marrow; staple (for stapler); (pencil) lead; stuffing; pith

hc: house; invariably; heart; insect
ks: When I think of **honey** , I think of a bee hive, which is a HOUSE that INVARIABLY has INSECTS.

v.mn: mandarin (esp. the satsuma mandarin (Citrus unshiu)); mandarin orange; tangerine

hs: You couldn't have asked for a better key word for this kanji! Just have a look at it: a perfect image of a **concave** lens (remembering, of course, that the kanji square off rounded things), complete with its own little  "cave." Now all you have to do is learn how to write it.
ks: The letter "U" is **concave** ly written.

v.mn: to be dented; to be indented; to yield; to give; to sink; to collapse; to cave i

kun: つや、なま.めかしい、あで.やか、つや.めく、なま.めく
m: glossy; luster; glaze; polish; charm; colorful; captivating
hc: bountiful; bend; beans; table; one; mouth; colour; color; bound up; mosaic
ks: To print something with bountiful colour, they use **glossy** paper.

v.mn: gloss; luster; lustre; shine; sheen; polish

m: pay respects; visit; ask; inquire; question; implore
hc: person; director; Ken Russel; clothes hanger; coat hanger; one; mouth
hs: This honorific form of _call on_ (Frame 534) is made up of: _person_ . . . _director_.
ks: _Mr. T_ is the _director_. You better **pay respects** to him!

v.mn: to call on someone; to call at a place; to pay a visit; to wait on someone

hc: moon; month; flesh; part of the body; cruise missile; missile; wind; crotch
ks: Where's my _flesh missile_? Between my **thighs** , naturally…

kun: にお.う、にお.い、にお.わせる
hc: bound up; spoon; sitting on the ground
hs: You will have to imagine "capturing" a favorite **aroma** by having it gagged and _bound up_  in a _spoon_.
ks: The BOUND UP SITTING MAN is going crazy because he can smell the **aroma** of fresh cinnamon buns, but can't get out of the ropes.

kun: もちあそ.ぶ、もてあそ.ぶ
m: play; take pleasure in; trifle with; make sport of
hc: king; jewel; ball; beginning; two; human legs
hs: If, at some aboriginal level, **Toys** Я Us, then the archetypal _ball_ must have been there at the _beginning_ , before evolving into beach balls, ping- pong balls, rugby balls, and marbles.
ks: The _ball_ was the only thing that people at the _beginning_ of time had to use as a **toy**. Poor cavemen.

hc: metal; gold; east; tree; wood; sun; day

hc: flowers; hood; stick; thread; spiderman; insect
hs: Though it's a good thing that the primitive for **cocoon** has been radically abbreviated from this, its full form as a kanji, the story it holds is a charming one. The silkworm ( _insect_ ) eats the leaves of the mulberry bush (the _flowers_ ), digests them and transforms them into _thread_ with which it spins about itself, in mystic wisdom, its own coffin (the _hood_ ). The dividing line that separates the two elements helps the picture of the little worm cutting itself off from contact with the outside world, but as a character stroke, it is a clear exception.
ks: This is a simple two-column manga showing the metamorphosis of a caterpillar into a **cocoon**. First, the caterpillar gets under a large flower (bees tend to hang around flowers, which scare away other predators). Then, in the first frame, we see the caterpillar ( _insect_ ). Then, a bar separating this frame from the next frame (remember that Japanese manga run the other way!), in which we see the _insect_ covered with _thread_ , making a beautiful **cocoon**.

hs: The key word refers to kanji that are written in full, squared form, as opposed to cursive writing which is more flowing andabbreviates some of the strokes. Here **block letters** are pictured as tidy rows of _trees_ that are _all_ the same size and can be read easily by _all_ , whatever calligraphic skills they possess.
ks: **Print-style writing** (as opposed to cursive): _All_ **print-style writing** looks rather _wooden_ to me. I prefer the fancy curves of cursive. (Note: Heisig gives this character the official keyword "block letters," but based on what I've read about the meaning of the character in the Yahoo! Japan Dictionary, I prefer "print-style writing").

kun: うっ.する、ふさ.ぐ、しげ.る
hs: Learning this character will take a little doing. Before we start, it is a good idea to keep the key word distinct from _melancholy_ (Frame 663), with which it combines to create the psychological condition of depression (a metaphor based on the hollow or sinking of the material _depression_ we met in Frame 1421). The picture of **gloom** begins with a small _grove_ of trees, under which we see a _crown_ made out of used _tin cans_. Next (the lower half of the kanji) we find a combination that appears only a few times in modern characters and only once in those treated in the volumes of _Remembering the Kanji_. It is composed of a _shovel_ with a _sheaf_ dotted with small _drops_ on all sides. Think of it as a large scoop _shovel_ attached to the front of a bulldozer, whose driver is singing: ♪  "Bulldozing in the _sheaves_ , bulldozing in the _sheaves_... " ♪ The _drops_ represent the chaff flying about as the _sheaves_ are tossed about by the heavy machinery. There is _someone sitting on the ground_ alongside, watching as the machinery makes its way around the neighboring farmland - and it's you, wearing your _tin-can crown_. You feel overwhelmed by the impending doom as the invasion of agro- business draws closer and closer to the _grove_ , the only patch of woods that remains of what once was a vast forest. The whole scene represents a re- _shaping_ of the natural environment that brings the heavy weight of **gloom** down upon you. A bit much, perhaps, but there are enough elements to allow for any number of other combinations.
ks: I'm depressed that this kanji has 29 strokes.

hs: The **holed gem** to which this kanji refers is a flat, circular object of about a foot in length, made of stone or glass and having a large hole in the middle. It's elements: _ketchup_ . . . _ball_.
ks: This kanji shows a policeman with a **holed gem** (which can be thought of as _jewelry_ , I guess), but he thinks it's a doughnut. For some reason though, he liked his doughnuts with ketchup on them, so he added some, and tried to take a bite. He lost a few teeth on the process.

The witches _piggy bank_ was cursed with a spell causing any thief who stole it's contents to fall ill. The _illness_ would result in the victim being covered from head to toe in a mass of **carbuncles**. Crime doesn't pay!

hs: _Bamboo_ . . . _float_. Note that the drawing of the primitive for _float_ uses the abbreviated form that has become standard in the general-use kanji and as we met it earlier (Frames 393-95). Until such time as typesetting fonts make the adjustment, only the older form has been approved. Here we have opted for the simpler, avant garde drawing.
ks: Note: the spelling should be **stationery** (= paper, office supplies), not **stationary** (= not moving). The error is in the original book.
